response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a capable full-stack staff engineer with breadth across web, mobile, and distributed systems, but falls far short of the CTO profile required here. The role demands executive leadership, a demonstrated track record of scaling engineering teams to 30+ people, and production-grade multi-agent AI systems — none of which are present. Her AI exposure is limited to early-stage MCP tooling started in April 2025, and her fintech experience (RIDE Capital) is shallow relative to the credit, payment, and working-capital infrastructure depth the role requires. There is no evidence of board-level communication, architectural governance at scale, or managing hybrid engineering delivery models.
strengths: Genuine breadth across distributed systems, event-driven architecture, and microservices (Dunnhumby, GISCollective), which provides relevant technical intuition
Early hands-on engagement with agentic AI infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, showing willingness to move into the space
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a history of teaching and knowledge-sharing that could support team culture
concerns: Zero demonstrated engineering leadership or people management — no team scaling, no org design, no evidence of developing senior technical leads or owning an engineering organization
No production multi-agent AI systems at meaningful scale; current AI work is very recent (< 2 months) and appears to be tooling/framework level, not architecture or operations
No fintech infrastructure depth — credit systems, real-time risk, payment flows, and regulatory compliance are entirely absent from the resume

key_factors.factor: Absent production agentic AI track record
